items.title: Woot’s Best of Tech sale now live with up to 86% off: Deals on PC gaming handhelds, Galaxy Tab, GoPro, and more from just $3.50
items.summary:
Woot has been kicking off back-to-back sale events this week, and it’s now offering a range of new deals as part of its Best of Tech sale. This time around, we’re looking at some notable offers on PC gaming handhelds, tablets, and more. In addition to steep markdowns on smaller accessories starting at just $3.50, Woot is also offering discounts of up to 86% on select big-ticket items such as TVs and cameras, making them well worth a look. Notably, you can also shop via the Woot app for an additional 10% off on all the items included in the sale. Similar to its Clearance events, one of which is still live with some huge offers, most of the deals featured as part of Woot’s updated Best of Tech sale are on brand-new products, though you’ll also find refurbished and factory-reconditioned picks at even lower prices. Head below for more details.

items.title: Aventon Memorial Day Sale offers Aventure M mid-drive all-terrain smart e-bike with $128 bundle at $2,699 low, more from $1,199
items.summary:
Aventon has launched its Memorial Day Sale that is dropping most of its smart e-bikes to their lowest prices, with most of them (even ones not receiving price cuts) also getting up to $206 in FREE gear. One of the most notable returns from the brand’s previous sale is Aventon’s most premium Aventure M Mid-Drive All-Terrain Smart e-bike coming with $128 in free gear back down at $2,699 shipped. Since its release in 2025, we mostly saw discounts to $2,799 until last month’s spring sale when it fell to $2,699 for the first time. Not only are you getting a second chance at this all-time low, but it also comes as the first time free bundled gear has been added on, too, making it the best price and overall deal we have tracked to date. Head below to learn more about this premium ride and browse the full lineup of e-bike deals.

items.title: EcoFlow’s 12,288Wh DELTA Pro Ultra X power station gets a Powerinsight 2 monitor and $1,649 exclusive savings to $7,199
items.summary:
We’ve got another exciting exclusive deal from Wellbots for our readers, giving you EcoFlow’s most capable DELTA Pro Ultra X Power Station with a Powerinsight 2 Monitor for $7,199.10 shipped, after using the code 10DPU9TO5 at checkout. This deal gives you the most expansive power solution (normally $10,597 MSRP and $8,499 at Wellbots) for the brand at $800 less than the price it is currently discounted to across multiple marketplaces, with the included monitor coming free of charge and valuing $349. In terms of the power station, this is the third-lowest price we have tracked, behind the exclusive $6,999 and $6,899 pricing we secured earlier in 2026. Now, with today’s exclusive deal, you’ll be saving $1,649 in total savings off the going rate (and $3,747 off the direct MSRP) while gaining one of the best whole-home backup solutions from one of our favorite brands.

items.title: UGREEN’s sleek new 2-in-1 Qi2.2 25W MagSafe charger is now just $40 with a launch deal
items.summary:
UGREEN’s official Amazon storefront is offering a nice launch deal on its all-new Qi2.2 25W 2-in-1 MagSafe charger. You can snag this latest model in both black and white finishes for $39.99 shipped. That works out to a 27% discount on its $55 list price it debuted with last month. This is essentially an upgraded Qi2.2 version of the 15W model we’ve featured previously, meaning you can now take advantage of faster 25W wireless charging speeds for quicker top-ups. It also sports a more distinctive design compared to many of the generic charging stands on the market, making it a nice option if you’re looking to add something a bit different to your desk or bedside setup.

items.title: AMD’s newest and fastest Ryzen 9850X3D gaming desktop processor just hit a new low at $459
items.summary:
We are now tracking a deal at Amazon that drops AMD’s latest Ryzen 7 9850X3D desktop processor down to $459 shipped. This is the newest chip in the brand’s X3D lineup, which hit the scene earlier this year carrying a $499 price tag. While you’re not looking at a massive discount on it right now, it’s still a noteworthy offer since it marks a new all-time low for the chip. It’s rare that we see AMD’s latest-generation processors receive meaningful discounts this soon after launch. The Ryzen 7 9800X3D, for instance, didn’t see its first major price cut until nearly a year after debuting. That makes today’s deal a notable one if you were already considering an upgrade to AMD’s newest gaming-focused CPU platform. Even saving a little here can free up extra room in your budget for smaller PC upgrades like additional case fans, storage, or peripherals such as a new keyboard or mouse. Head below for more details
